```text
# Review: Phase 10 and Phase 13 Specs

## Phase 10: `10.drafts-dispatch-and-outbound-gates.spec.md`

### 1) Ambiguities/contradictions

- `eligible states` for `send now` are not explicitly enumerated in the spec body/AC text.  
  Ref: `L74-L75`, `L108-L111`
- “valid `draft_id` + `integration_attempt_id`” is underspecified; validity does not define ownership binding (`workspace_id`, `integration_id`, current draft status, not-expired attempt).  
  Ref: `L28`, `L85`, `L120-L122`
- “managed integration endpoints” is not defined as a concrete registry/scope, so bypass surface is ambiguous.  
  Ref: `L27-L29`, `L83-L87`
- Policy lineage persistence is required but the required field set is not explicitly listed in this phase spec.  
  Ref: `L31`, `L128-L130`
- “exactly-once behavior” is required, but the logical attempt boundary and replay behavior are not defined in ACs.  
  Ref: `L9`, `L100`, `L148`
- Pending/Processed semantics are in scope, but tab-to-status mapping is not specified in this phase (left implicit).  
  Ref: `L15`, `L69-L71`, `L75`

### 2) Missing assumptions

- Assumes existing draft rows can be migrated safely to new lifecycle/status semantics without backfill rules.  
  Ref: `L13-L15`, `L43`
- Assumes authorization model for `approve` vs `send now` already exists and is enforceable at API boundary.  
  Ref: `L16-L20`, `L74-L75`
- Assumes runtime can reliably identify all managed integration endpoints (single source of truth).  
  Ref: `L27-L29`, `L41-L42`
- Assumes dispatcher can perform cross-entity binding checks (`draft`, `run`, `integration_attempt`, workspace type) atomically.  
  Ref: `L28-L29`, `L85-L87`
- Assumes test harness can run outbound-path checks without real provider side effects.  
  Ref: `L120-L123`, `L132-L141`

### 3) Acceptance/validation gaps

- No explicit transition matrix artifact is required by AC-01; “deterministic” is not measurable without enumerated transitions.  
  Ref: `L104-L107`
- No AC for forged-but-structurally-valid identifiers (wrong workspace, wrong integration, stale attempt ID).  
  Ref: `L120-L123`
- No AC verifying “no side effects” in storage/provider when dispatch is blocked/revalidation fails.  
  Ref: `L116-L119`
- No concurrency/race AC for approve-edit-send overlap and duplicate send requests.  
  Ref: `L112-L115`, `L148`
- AC-07 does not require immutable lineage/provenance checks (only presence).  
  Ref: `L128-L130`
- Validation block is broad and does not include explicit expected outputs or named security-focused test selectors.  
  Ref: `L132-L141`

### 4) Concrete edits (what to add/change)

- Add a new subsection under `In Scope`: `Draft status and tab mapping (normative)` with explicit mapping and allowed transition table.
- Update Step 2 + AC-02 text to explicitly state: `send now` is allowed only from `approved` and `auto_approved`.
- Expand Step 4 text to define `valid context` as: IDs exist, match same draft/run/workspace/integration, draft status is dispatch-eligible, and attempt is active.
- Add a new AC: forged ID mismatch is rejected (`draft_id`/`integration_attempt_id` from different workspace or integration).
- Add a new AC: blocked/revalidation-failed dispatch performs zero outbound writes and emits deterministic failure code.
- Expand AC-07 to require exact lineage keys: `policy_id`, `policy_version`, `decision_id`, `decision_reason_code`.
- Add a new AC: concurrent `send now` requests for same draft produce at most one external dispatch result.
- Replace generic validation commands with command + expected result lines for draft-gate tests (unit, integration, and e2e selectors).

## Phase 13: `13.security-governance-hardening.spec.md`

### 1) Ambiguities/contradictions

- “managed endpoints” remains undefined here as well; enforcement scope is not machine-testable without endpoint inventory rules.  
  Ref: `L13`, `L60-L63`
- Step 3 says “reject incomplete audit writes,” but does not define fail behavior (fail request, fallback queue, or hard error) for runtime safety.  
  Ref: `L69-L73`
- Report allowlist enforcement is required, but allowlist ownership/source-of-truth/update policy is unspecified.  
  Ref: `L20-L22`, `L74-L79`
- Non-origin hard-block AC duplicates phase 10 behavior but phase dependency/order is not stated, causing ownership ambiguity.  
  Ref: `L103-L106`

### 2) Missing assumptions

- Assumes all outbound paths are interceptable by one runtime gate, including legacy codepaths and desktop-layer helpers.  
  Ref: `L13`, `L58-L63`
- Assumes diagnostics/crash/tool outputs are centrally controllable for redaction before persistence/export.  
  Ref: `L14-L18`, `L64-L67`
- Assumes canary tests can deterministically observe all leak surfaces in CI/local runs.  
  Ref: `L66-L67`, `L91-L94`
- Assumes audit schema enforcement can reject writes without creating availability regressions.  
  Ref: `L71-L73`, `L95-L98`

### 3) Acceptance/validation gaps

- AC-01 does not include forged-context mismatch cases (valid-looking but wrong-bound IDs).  
  Ref: `L87-L90`
- AC-02 does not require encoded/derived secret checks (URL-encoded/base64/partial token patterns).  
  Ref: `L91-L94`
- AC-03 does not require validation of policy lineage values for every policy/dispatch event type, only “incomplete events rejected.”  
  Ref: `L95-L98`
- AC-04 does not require explicit machine-readable reason code verification for blocked payload/destination cases.  
  Ref: `L99-L102`
- Validation commands lack explicit security-focused selectors and expected pass criteria per command output.  
  Ref: `L107-L117`
- Desktop surface is only typechecked, not behavior-tested for logging redaction.  
  Ref: `L115-L116`

### 4) Concrete edits (what to add/change)

- Add a phase dependency note in `Baseline Assumptions`: “Phase 10 AC-01..AC-07 completed before this phase starts.”
- Add a normative definition section: `Managed endpoint inventory and matching rules` (host/path/method classification and update ownership).
- Amend Step 3 with explicit failure policy: when audit lineage is missing, operation fails closed with deterministic error code and no outbound dispatch.
- Add AC for forged-context bypass attempts (`draft_id` and `integration_attempt_id` mismatch by workspace/integration).
- Expand AC-02 to include encoded leak vectors (base64/url-encoded/token-fragment checks) across diagnostics, crash dumps, tool output, and exported reports.
- Expand AC-04 to assert explicit reason codes for allowlist violations and verify zero outbound side effects.
- Add desktop runtime redaction behavior tests (not just `typecheck`) in validation commands.
- Replace generic validation command block with command + expected observable output + pass/fail rule lines, per GUIDE requirements.
```

````text
# 04 Spec: Runtime Contracts and State Machines

## Goal

Establish deterministic runtime contracts for `Run`, `Operation`, `Draft`, and audit/event records so all later phases build on a single, testable persistence model.

## Why This Phase Exists

The PRD now defines strict lifecycle and policy behavior. Implementation should not start with ad hoc state updates. We need a contract-first baseline that guarantees:

- canonical statuses and failure codes
- legal transition edges
- idempotent attempt identities
- auditable event lineage

Without this phase, later queue/integration/draft features will diverge and become hard to validate.

## In Scope

- Add/extend DB schema for:
  - `run` with canonical status enum and reason/failure fields
  - `operation` with linkage fields and status
  - `draft` lifecycle fields and provenance
  - `integration_attempt` identity and replay fields
  - audit/event table with required baseline fields
- Add enum and transition-guard layer in runtime services.
- Add canonical failure code enum values from PRD.
- Enforce illegal-transition rejection at service layer.
- Add deterministic indexes/constraints:
  - uniqueness for `(run_id, integration_attempt_id)`
  - queue ordering keys
  - dedupe-oriented lookup indexes needed by later phases
- Add migration(s) and startup schema compatibility checks.
- Add SDK/runtime types if server contracts are changed.

## Out of Scope

- JJ bootstrap/workspace operations.
- Workflow execution logic.
- Integration queue processor.
- Draft dispatch behavior.
- Cron/signal trigger behavior.
- New user-facing screens.

## Baseline Assumptions

- DB hard-stop behavior on DB failure remains required.
- Drizzle/schema system remains the source of truth for runtime tables.
- Existing runtime can accept additive migrations without destructive reset.
- UUIDv7/ULID generation utilities already exist or can be added in this phase.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Existing storage schema and migration surfaces:
  - [storage/schema.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/storage/schema.ts)
  - [storage/schema.sql.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/storage/schema.sql.ts)
  - [storage/db.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts)
- Existing session/runtime status surfaces:
  - [session/status.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/session/status.ts)
  - [session/index.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/session/index.ts)
- Existing server route surfaces:
  - [server/routes/session.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/session.ts)
  - [server/routes/project.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/project.ts)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Add Canonical Runtime Enums and Failure Codes

- Introduce shared enums for:
  - `run.status`
  - `operation.status`
  - `draft.status`
  - standardized failure/reason codes
- Ensure enums are reused by DB schema + service validation + API serialization.

### Step 2: Extend DB Schema and Constraints

- Add required columns and indexes for PRD contracts:
  - immutable queue ordering key
  - integration attempt identity and replay fields
  - policy lineage fields on relevant events
- Enforce uniqueness and nullability constraints.
- Provide deterministic default values where required.

### Step 3: Add Transition Guard Service

- Add centralized transition function per entity.
- Reject illegal transitions with machine-readable errors.
- Record mandatory terminal metadata (`reason`, `failure_code`, timestamps).

### Step 4: Add Audit/Event Baseline Writer

- Add typed event creation utility that validates required fields by event category.
- Enforce schema-level redaction rules for payload shape (no secret-like free-form fields in baseline typed payload).

### Step 5: Validation and Regression Coverage

- Add unit tests for state transitions and forbidden edges.
- Add migration tests for schema evolution.
- Add persistence tests for uniqueness/constraint enforcement.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Canonical enums exist and are reused across schema and runtime state transitions.
  - Verification: unit tests import the same enum source in DB/runtime modules.
  - Pass condition: no duplicated ad hoc enum strings in transition logic.

- `AC-02` Illegal transitions are rejected deterministically.
  - Verification: unit tests attempt invalid edges for `run`, `operation`, `draft`.
  - Pass condition: each invalid transition returns machine-readable error and no DB mutation.

- `AC-03` Required uniqueness constraints are enforced.
  - Verification: persistence test inserts duplicate `(run_id, integration_attempt_id)`.
  - Pass condition: second insert fails with deterministic constraint error.

- `AC-04` Required audit baseline fields are enforced for applicable event types.
  - Verification: tests attempt writes missing required policy lineage fields for policy/dispatch events.
  - Pass condition: write rejected with explicit validation error.

- `AC-05` Migration is forward-applicable on existing local DB state.
  - Verification command:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test -- storage
```
  - Pass condition: migration tests pass and no destructive reset is required.

- `AC-06` Typecheck/test baseline for touched packages is green.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- Schema churn may require follow-up migrations if later phases discover missing fields.
- Overly strict constraints can block transitional writes during rollout.
- Poor event payload typing can create frequent migration noise.
- Transition guards can be bypassed if direct table writes remain in legacy paths.
````

````text
# 05 Spec: JJ Runtime Foundation

## Goal

Implement the app-managed JJ runtime layer (bootstrap, safe command wrappers, isolated run workspace lifecycle, and idempotent cleanup) without yet implementing workflow execution semantics.

## Why This Phase Exists

Later phases rely on JJ as checkpoint/runtime sidecar. If JJ interaction is not standardized first, command behavior, bootstrap mode, and cleanup semantics will drift and become unsafe.

## In Scope

- Add JJ runtime adapter with:
  - bundled runtime invocation path
  - canonical read-only command wrapper
  - canonical mutating command wrapper with telemetry hooks
- Implement bootstrap matrix behavior:
  - `.jj` exists
  - `.git` exists and `.jj` absent (colocated init)
  - no VCS metadata
- Add run workspace creation/forget/delete helpers.
- Implement idempotent cleanup behavior and telemetry outcomes.
- Implement poll/telemetry command policy:
  - read-only flows must use `--ignore-working-copy --at-op=@` where supported.
- Add test seams for JJ command policy without introducing full run execution.

## Out of Scope

- Run lifecycle orchestration.
- Queueing/integration logic.
- Reconciliation loop.
- User-facing history/draft UI.
- Cron/signal behavior.

## Baseline Assumptions

- Bundled JJ binary/path mechanism is available in desktop runtime.
- Existing project/workspace abstractions can attach JJ adapter context.
- Local filesystem writes are allowed inside workspace path boundaries.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Existing VCS/project surfaces:
  - [project/vcs.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/project/vcs.ts)
  - [project/project.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts)
  - [project/instance.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/project/instance.ts)
- Existing storage/workspace primitives:
  - [control-plane/workspace.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts)
  - [control-plane/workspace.sql.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.sql.ts)
- Existing app runtime adapters:
  - [runtime-adapters.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/utils/runtime-adapters.ts)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Introduce JJ Adapter Contracts

- Add `jj` adapter interface with explicit methods:
  - bootstrap
  - read-only command execution
  - mutating command execution
  - run workspace create/forget/delete
- Include structured result object with exit code, stderr classification, and telemetry tags.

### Step 2: Implement Bootstrap Matrix

- Implement deterministic bootstrap behavior for:
  - `.jj` present: no-op
  - `.git` only: initialize colocated JJ
  - neither: initialize JJ
- Ensure identity local-write rules from PRD are respected.

### Step 3: Add Read-Only Command Guard

- Centralize read-only wrapper adding canonical flags where command supports them.
- Reject or rewrite unsafe polling commands from caller paths.

### Step 4: Add Run Workspace Lifecycle Utilities

- Create run workspace helper with deterministic naming.
- Add cleanup helper:
  - forget workspace metadata first
  - delete path second
  - treat missing artifacts as success with warning telemetry

### Step 5: Test and Validation

- Add unit tests for bootstrap decision logic.
- Add unit/integration tests for command policy wrapper behavior.
- Add cleanup idempotency tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Bootstrap behavior is deterministic across `.jj`, `.git`, and empty directories.
  - Verification: matrix tests for all three states.
  - Pass condition: resulting JJ state matches spec and no destructive side effects occur.

- `AC-02` Read-only polling wrapper enforces canonical no-snapshot policy.
  - Verification: tests execute read polling repeatedly.
  - Pass condition: no unintended JJ operation mutations are produced by polling paths.

- `AC-03` Cleanup helper is idempotent.
  - Verification: run cleanup twice with already-forgotten/deleted artifacts.
  - Pass condition: second run succeeds with warning telemetry, not hard failure.

- `AC-04` Run workspace creation/cleanup APIs expose deterministic structured results.
  - Verification: unit tests validate result schema and telemetry fields.
  - Pass condition: no raw string-only error propagation.

- `AC-05` Touched package validation passes.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/desktop typecheck
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- JJ behavior differences across versions can invalidate command expectations.
- Wrapper overreach can block legitimate commands needed later.
- Cleanup handling may hide real failures if warning/error boundaries are too loose.
- Colocated bootstrap may behave differently on unusual repo layouts.
````

````text
# 06 Spec: Workflow and Library Authoring with Validation

## Goal

Deliver authoring/parsing/validation for workflow and library resources so users can create runnable definitions with deterministic validation outcomes before execution features are enabled.

## Why This Phase Exists

Execution and trigger features depend on valid resource definitions. We need a stable authoring and validation contract first, including non-origin capability boundaries and collision rules.

## In Scope

- Add/extend YAML schemas for:
  - workflows
  - library resources (`query`, `script`, `prompt_template`)
- Implement parser/validator pipeline with explicit error codes.
- Enforce workspace capability constraints:
  - Origin vs standard workspace support rules
- Implement reference integrity validation:
  - missing resources
  - invalid kinds
  - broken links
- Implement knowledge-base import collision policy:
  - interactive: `Replace` / `Create copy` / `Cancel`
  - non-interactive (`cron`, `signal`): deterministic `Create copy` + notify
- Add UI validation surfacing hooks and runtime non-runnable handling for invalid definitions.

## Out of Scope

- Running workflows.
- Queue/integration behavior.
- Draft dispatch.
- Cron/signal scheduling runtime.

## Baseline Assumptions

- `.origin/workflows/*.yaml` and `.origin/library/*.yaml` remain canonical file storage.
- Existing config/markdown helpers can be reused for parsing and diagnostics formatting.
- Workspace type is already available and immutable in runtime state.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Config and schema infrastructure:
  - [config/config.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts)
  - [config/markdown.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/config/markdown.ts)
- File/workspace route surfaces:

  - [server/routes/workspace.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/workspace.ts)
- App validation and settings surfaces:
  - [context/settings.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/context/settings.tsx)
  - [context/layout.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/context/layout.tsx)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Define Workflow/Library Schemas

- Add versioned schema definitions and parsers.
- Validate required fields and enum values.
- Attach machine-readable error codes for every validation failure class.

### Step 2: Implement Validation Service

- Build validation entrypoint that returns:
  - normalized resource object
  - error list with code/path/message
  - runnable flag
- Enforce non-origin capability rules.

### Step 3: Implement Reference and Collision Logic

- Validate references between workflow and library assets.
- Add deterministic collision handling strategy for knowledge-base imports.

### Step 4: Integrate Validation Surfacing

- Ensure invalid definitions never execute.

### Step 5: Test Coverage

- Parser and schema tests.
- Reference integrity tests.
- Workspace capability boundary tests.
- Collision policy tests for interactive and automated contexts.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Valid definitions parse and normalize successfully.
  - Verification: unit tests for each schema kind with representative fixtures.
  - Pass condition: normalized output matches expected shape.

- `AC-02` Invalid definitions are non-runnable with deterministic error codes.
  - Verification: invalid fixture matrix.
  - Pass condition: each invalid case emits stable code/path and runnable=false.

- `AC-03` Non-origin workspace capability constraints are enforced.
  - Verification: tests attempt unsupported resource usage in standard workspace.
  - Pass condition: deterministic validation error and non-runnable result.

- `AC-04` Reference integrity validation catches missing/broken links.
  - Verification: fixtures with missing resource and wrong kind references.
  - Pass condition: errors include reference path and reason code.

- `AC-05` Knowledge-base collision handling is deterministic.
  - Verification:
    - interactive flow test for `Replace`/`Create copy`/`Cancel`
    - automated flow test for deterministic `Create copy`
  - Pass condition: resulting file state and notifications match policy.

- `AC-06` Validation command suite passes.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- Schema evolution may require migration helpers if early definitions are incomplete.
- Validation strictness can block legitimate legacy files unexpectedly.
- Error-code instability can make downstream e2e tests brittle.
- Collision logic can become inconsistent across platforms if path normalization is weak.
````

````text
# 07 Spec: Manual Run Execution

## Goal

Ship end-to-end manual workflow execution with session linkage and deterministic run lifecycle transitions, without yet introducing queued integration or automated triggers.

## Why This Phase Exists

This is the first user-visible execution slice. It validates the run model and session behavior before concurrency/recovery complexity is added.

## In Scope

- Manual trigger entrypoint for workflows.
- Create one new chat session per top-level run.
- Execute run in isolated run workspace.
- End-of-run validation flow:
  - success path
  - invalid-output repair loop in same session
- No-change vs change outcome classification.
- Persist run records with canonical lifecycle states.
- Record operation only when file changes exist.
- Always perform run workspace cleanup on terminal outcomes.

## Out of Scope

- Integration queue/serialization and replay logic.
- Draft creation/dispatch.
- Cron and signal triggers.
- Hidden debug/reconciliation notification UX.

## Baseline Assumptions

- Runtime contracts (Phase 04) and JJ foundation (Phase 05) are available.
- Workflow/library validation (Phase 06) can mark runnable/non-runnable before execution.
- Existing session creation and prompt plumbing can be reused.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Session processing surfaces:
  - [session/processor.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts)
  - [session/index.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/session/index.ts)
- Prompt submit flow:
  - [prompt-input/submit.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/components/prompt-input/submit.ts)
- Session UI/route surfaces:
  - [pages/session.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/pages/session.tsx)
  - [pages/layout.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/pages/layout.tsx)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Add Manual Run Start Endpoint/Command

- Validate workflow is runnable.
- Create run record + linked session.
- Create isolated run workspace.

### Step 2: Execute Run in Session Context

- Run top-level instructions via base orchestration flow.
- Keep context in same run session thread.

### Step 3: Validation and Repair Loop

- Run final validation checks.
- If invalid, prompt same session for repair and retry validation.
- Keep retry attempts auditable in run events.

### Step 4: Persist Outcome and Cleanup

- If no file changes: `completed_no_change`, no operation row.
- If file changes: mark integration-ready state and create operation candidate metadata.
- Cleanup run workspace for all terminal outcomes.

### Step 5: Test Coverage

- Manual run happy path.
- Validation-fail then repair path.
- No-change path.
- Cleanup on success/failure/cancel.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Manual run creation always creates a new linked session.
  - Verification: run start test asserts distinct `session_id` per run.
  - Pass condition: each manual run has a new session thread.

- `AC-02` Run lifecycle transitions are legal and deterministic.
  - Verification: unit/integration tests around state transitions.
  - Pass condition: no illegal transition writes.

- `AC-03` Validation-fail path repairs in same session.
  - Verification: test fixture forcing invalid final state.
  - Pass condition: repair prompt/events occur in same `session_id`, then run resolves.

- `AC-04` No-change runs do not create Operations entries.
  - Verification: run with no file mutations.
  - Pass condition: run ends `completed_no_change` and operations query returns zero new rows.

- `AC-05` All terminal outcomes trigger cleanup.
  - Verification: success/failure/cancel test matrix.
  - Pass condition: no orphaned run workspace remains.

- `AC-06` Validation commands pass.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e:local -- e2e/session
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- Repair loops can become noisy if validation conditions are too broad.
- Session linkage bugs can create orphan run/session records.
- Cleanup race conditions can leave stale run workspaces.
- Early execution path may expose latent prompt/tool regressions.
````

</details>

````text
# 08 Spec: Integration Queue and Crash-Safe Recovery

## Goal

Implement serialized per-workspace integration with immutable queue ordering, idempotent integration attempts, stale-base replay, and deterministic cancel/restart outcomes.

## Why This Phase Exists

Manual execution exists after Phase 07, but checkpoint integration safety is the highest-risk part of correctness. This phase isolates queue/recovery semantics before draft dispatch and automated triggers are added.

## In Scope

- Per-workspace integration serialization lock.
- FIFO queue by immutable `ready_for_integration_at` + `run_id` tiebreaker.
- Integration attempt lifecycle with `(run_id, integration_attempt_id)` uniqueness.
- Atomic integration boundary enforcement:
  - JJ mutation + DB linkage commit
- Crash recovery behavior:
  - recover in-flight attempt
  - finalize post-mutation pre-DB crash path
- Stale-base detection and one replay attempt (`replay_index`).
- Deterministic cancellation semantics during integrating/reconciling.
- Failure-code emission for replay exhaustion and cleanup failures.

## Out of Scope

- Draft dispatch to external integrations.
- Cron/signal trigger creation.
- Long-running debug reminder UX.

## Baseline Assumptions

- Phase 04 state models and Phase 05 JJ adapter are available.
- Phase 07 creates integration-ready run outputs.
- Run queue persistence is DB-backed and recoverable on startup.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Queue/util foundations:
  - [util/queue.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/util/queue.ts)
  - [scheduler/index.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/scheduler/index.ts)
- Session/run processing surfaces:
  - [session/processor.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts)
- Project/workspace runtime surfaces:
  - [project/project.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts)
  - [control-plane/workspace.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Implement Queue Persistence and Ordering

- Persist immutable `ready_for_integration_at` once on enqueue.
- Enforce deterministic dequeue ordering.
- Preserve ordering across retries and restarts.

### Step 2: Implement Workspace Integration Locking

- Add per-workspace lock acquisition/release path.
- Ensure only one integrating run per workspace at a time.

### Step 3: Implement Attempt Identity and Atomic Boundary

- Create `integration_attempt_id` before apply.
- Enforce atomic commit boundary:
  - apply JJ mutation
  - commit DB operation linkage

### Step 4: Implement Recovery and Replay

- On startup:
  - detect unfinished attempts
  - recover/finalize without duplicate mutation
- Detect stale-base via `integration_head_change_id_before_apply` mismatch.
- Replay once via update-stale + reapply.
- If replay fails/exhausted, set `stale_base_replay_exhausted`.

### Step 5: Implement Cancel Edge Semantics

- Pre-integration cancel => `canceled` and queue removal.
- In integration/reconcile:
  - mark `cancel_requested`
  - finish current atomic step
  - resolve to `completed` if committed, else `canceled`.
- Timeout/cancel race: first terminal write wins.

### Step 6: Test Matrix

- concurrency ordering
- crash injection points
- replay path
- cancel race path

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Queue ordering is immutable under retries/restarts.
  - Verification: integration queue tests with forced retries/restarts.
  - Pass condition: dequeue order remains based on initial key.

- `AC-02` Only one integrating run per workspace is active at any time.
  - Verification: concurrent run test in same workspace.
  - Pass condition: lock prevents parallel integration.

- `AC-03` Crash after JJ mutation before DB finalize does not double-apply.
  - Verification: crash-injection test at post-mutation checkpoint.
  - Pass condition: exactly one effective mutation and one finalized operation linkage.

- `AC-04` Stale-base replay occurs at most once.
  - Verification: force head drift, then drift again.
  - Pass condition: one replay attempt then terminal `stale_base_replay_exhausted`.

- `AC-05` Cancel during integration follows deterministic outcome contract.
  - Verification: cancel timing matrix tests.
  - Pass condition: committed integration resolves `completed` + marker; uncommitted resolves `canceled`.

- `AC-06` Validation commands pass.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e:local -- e2e/session
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- Locking bugs can deadlock integration processing.
- Recovery path mistakes can silently duplicate mutations.
- Replay heuristics may misclassify drift cause.
- Cancel race handling can regress if terminal precedence is not centralized.
````

</details>

````text
# 09 Spec: History, Runs, and Operations UX

## Goal

Implement the History surface (`Runs` + `Operations`) with deterministic cross-linking, provenance filtering, and debug visibility behavior aligned with runtime contracts.

## Why This Phase Exists

Users need observable execution and file-change history before outbound actions and automation triggers are expanded. This phase makes runtime behavior inspectable and reviewable.

## In Scope

- History view with tabs:
  - Runs
  - Operations
- Default Operations filter:
  - app-authored only by default
  - explicit toggle for user-authored entries
- Minimum cross-link contract:
  - Operation -> Open run
  - Run with changes -> Open operation
  - Run with drafts -> Open draft(s)
- Debug run visibility controls:
  - hidden by default
  - reveal via `Show Debug Sessions`
  - deep-link behavior from notification action
- Duplicate-event row rendering in Runs:
  - visible as non-error event row
  - excluded from run execution counters

## Out of Scope

- Draft approval/sending UX details (Phase 10).
- Trigger scheduling UX (Phase 11).
- Reconciliation reminder delivery logic (Phase 12).

## Baseline Assumptions

- Runtime emits stable linkage IDs and statuses from earlier phases.
- Existing sidebar/session layout can host History navigation.
- Existing titlebar/history component can be extended.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Existing history-related UI surfaces:
  - [titlebar-history.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/components/titlebar-history.ts)
  - [titlebar-history.test.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/components/titlebar-history.test.ts)
- Layout/sidebar integration surfaces:
  - [pages/layout.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/pages/layout.tsx)
  - [pages/layout/sidebar-items.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/pages/layout/sidebar-items.tsx)
- Notification/deep-link surfaces:
  - [context/notification.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/context/notification.tsx)
  - [utils/notification-click.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/utils/notification-click.ts)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Build History Data Queries

- Add read models for runs/operations with stable sorting and pagination.
- Include provenance and linkage metadata in query response shapes.

### Step 2: Implement Runs Tab

- Render execution rows and duplicate-event rows distinctly.
- Exclude duplicate-event rows from execution counters.
- Support debug filtering controls.

### Step 3: Implement Operations Tab

- Default app-authored filter.
- Optional include-user-edits toggle.
- Render source markers and links.

### Step 4: Implement Cross-Link Actions

- Add deterministic navigation actions:
  - Open run
  - Open operation
  - Open draft(s)
- Ensure missing-link states are handled gracefully.

### Step 5: Add Debug Deep-Link Behavior

- `Open debug session` actions auto-enable debug visibility and focus target entry.

### Step 6: Validation Coverage

- component tests
- data query tests
- e2e navigation and filter tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` History has working `Runs` and `Operations` tabs with stable ordering.
  - Verification: component/e2e tests.
  - Pass condition: deterministic order and tab switching behavior.

- `AC-02` Operations default filter shows app-authored only.
  - Verification: seeded mixed data test.
  - Pass condition: user-authored entries appear only when toggle enabled.

- `AC-03` Cross-link actions satisfy minimum contract.
  - Verification: seeded run/operation/draft linkage fixtures.
  - Pass condition: all required links open target views correctly.

- `AC-04` Debug visibility behavior is deterministic.
  - Verification: hidden-by-default + deep-link activation tests.
  - Pass condition: deep-link enables debug visibility and focuses target row.

- `AC-05` Duplicate-event rows are visible but excluded from execution counters.
  - Verification: seeded duplicate-event fixtures.
  - Pass condition: row visible with dedicated styling and execution counters unchanged.

- `AC-06` Validation commands pass.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e:local -- e2e/history
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- High-cardinality history data can cause list performance regressions.
- Link consistency can regress if IDs are missing in some legacy rows.
- Filter defaults can confuse users if toggle state persistence is inconsistent.
- Debug visibility deep-linking can produce focus bugs on list refresh.
````

````text
# 10 Spec: Draft Dispatch and Outbound Safety Gates

## Goal

Implement the full Draft lifecycle, explicit approve/send model, dispatch revalidation, and strict runtime outbound gating so external side effects are always controlled and auditable.

## Why This Phase Exists

Outbound safety is core product trust. This phase enforces that all external writes pass through Draft controls with policy lineage and exactly-once semantics.

## In Scope

- Draft lifecycle UI/runtime:
  - `pending`, `blocked`, `approved`, `auto_approved`, `sent`, `rejected`, `failed`
- Pending/Processed tab semantics.
- Explicit user actions:
  - approve (ready-to-send)
  - send now
  - reject
  - edit
- Approval invalidation on material edit.
- Dispatch-time revalidation:
  - policy
  - destination/action constraints
  - integration enabled-state
  - auth health
- Strict runtime outbound gate:
  - managed integration endpoints accept writes only from dispatcher with valid `draft_id` + `integration_attempt_id`
- Non-origin outbound hard rejection (`workspace_policy_blocked`) with user-visible remediation outcome.
- Auto-approve behavior and policy-default-deny handling.
- Policy lineage fields persisted for policy/dispatch decisions.

## Out of Scope

- Cron/signal scheduling mechanics (Phase 11).
- Debug reminder flow (Phase 12).
- Advanced governance retention/tamper-evidence enhancements.

## Baseline Assumptions

- Integration adapters can expose a single dispatch entrypoint controlled by backend.
- Policy engine scaffolding exists or can be added in this phase.
- Draft rows and run linkage tables exist from earlier phases.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Settings and provider surfaces:
  - [settings-providers.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/components/settings-providers.tsx)
  - [context/settings.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/context/settings.tsx)
- Permission/approval-like interaction surfaces:
  - [context/permission.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/context/permission.tsx)
  - [context/permission-auto-respond.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/context/permission-auto-respond.ts)
- Server/provider routes:
  - [server/routes/provider.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/provider.ts)
  - [provider/auth.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/provider/auth.ts)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Implement Draft Lifecycle Runtime

- Add deterministic transition handlers.
- Enforce tab mapping semantics and terminal/non-terminal states.

### Step 2: Implement Approve/Send Split

- Separate `approve` and `send now` actions in API/UI.
- Keep `approved`/`auto_approved` in Pending until terminal dispatch outcome.

### Step 3: Implement Invalidation and Revalidation

- Material edit after approval resets to `pending`.
- Dispatch revalidates policy/auth/enabled-state/destination.
- Fail closed to `blocked` with reason code when revalidation fails.

### Step 4: Implement Strict Dispatcher Gate

- Reject direct outbound writes without valid draft context identifiers.
- Enforce Origin-only workspace outbound gate.

### Step 5: Implement Auto-Approve Rules

- Auto-approve default OFF.
- Apply adapter policy constraints.
- Policy-eval failure -> `blocked` default-deny.

### Step 6: Test Coverage

- lifecycle transitions
- edit invalidation
- revalidation failures
- non-origin hard-block
- outbound exactly-once behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Draft state machine is deterministic and rejects illegal transitions.
  - Verification: transition matrix unit tests.
  - Pass condition: all invalid transitions fail and valid transitions succeed.

- `AC-02` Approve and send are separate actions.
  - Verification: UI/service tests.
  - Pass condition: `approve` does not dispatch; `send now` dispatches from eligible states only.

- `AC-03` Material edit invalidates approval.
  - Verification: approve draft, edit material field, inspect state.
  - Pass condition: state resets to `pending` and requires re-approval.

- `AC-04` Dispatch revalidation fail-closes.
  - Verification: revoke auth/disable integration/change policy after approval.
  - Pass condition: dispatch becomes `blocked` with explicit reason and no external write.

- `AC-05` Direct outbound calls without draft context are rejected.
  - Verification: integration endpoint test without valid `draft_id` + `integration_attempt_id`.
  - Pass condition: call rejected and audit event emitted.

- `AC-06` Non-origin outbound attempts are rejected and visible.
  - Verification: attempt outbound action in standard workspace.
  - Pass condition: deterministic `workspace_policy_blocked` outcome with remediation hint.

- `AC-07` Policy lineage fields are persisted for allow/deny/dispatch decisions.
  - Verification: audit/event query tests.
  - Pass condition: required lineage fields are present.

- `AC-08` Validation commands pass.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e:local -- e2e/drafts
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- Dispatch gate bypasses can occur if legacy outbound codepaths remain.
- Approval invalidation logic can over-trigger and hurt UX if field classification is wrong.
- Policy lineage gaps can break auditability claims.
- Async dispatch race conditions can produce duplicate sends without strict idempotency checks.
````

````text
# 11 Spec: Cron and Signal Trigger Engine

## Goal

Implement automated workflow entry via cron and signal triggers with deterministic dedupe/retry/missed-run behavior and clear run recording semantics.

## Why This Phase Exists

After manual execution and outbound safety are stable, automation can be enabled safely. Trigger semantics must be deterministic to avoid duplicate work, silent drops, or confusing history.

## In Scope

- Cron trigger engine:
  - user timezone scheduling
  - missed-slot behavior (`skipped` + notify)
  - retry policy with retryable/non-retryable classes
- Signal trigger engine:
  - Origin workspace only
  - no backfill on enable
  - dedupe key enforcement
  - duplicate-event recording
  - fallback hash behavior when provider event ID is missing
- Trigger-to-run creation pipeline:
  - new session per top-level run
  - run metadata includes trigger source
- Runs visibility for trigger outcomes:
  - skipped cron slots
  - duplicate signal event rows (`Ignored duplicate signal`)

## Out of Scope

- Reconciliation long-running reminder UX polish.
- Governance retention tier expansion.
- New integration adapter development.

## Baseline Assumptions

- Workflow definitions and manual execution path are stable.
- Deduplication persistence fields exist.
- Notification surfaces exist for skip/duplicate info.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Scheduler and event utility surfaces:
  - [scheduler/index.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/scheduler/index.ts)
  - [bus-event.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/bus/bus-event.ts)
  - [util/signal.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/util/signal.ts)
- Server routes relevant to event ingress:
  - [server/routes/global.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/global.ts)
  - [server/routes/project.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/project.ts)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Implement Cron Scheduling and Missed-Slot Logic

- Schedule by user timezone.
- On missed windows, record `skipped` run outcome and notify.
- Apply retry policy only for retryable failures.

### Step 2: Implement Signal Ingress and Dedupe

- Enforce dedupe uniqueness key.
- If provider event ID absent, compute fallback hash from canonical adapter fields.
- Duplicate event records no run execution and creates visible duplicate-event row.

### Step 3: Trigger Metadata and Session Creation

- Ensure each automated run creates a new session.
- Persist trigger metadata and linkage IDs.

### Step 4: Notification/Visibility Integration

- Wire missed/duplicate outcomes into Runs surface and notifications.

### Step 5: Test Coverage

- cron scheduling and missed-slot
- retry classification
- signal dedupe and fallback hash
- no-backfill behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Missed cron slots create deterministic skipped outcomes.
  - Verification: simulate offline/missed interval.
  - Pass condition: one `skipped` run row per missed slot with reason metadata and notification.

- `AC-02` Retry policy applies only to retryable classes.
  - Verification: failure-class matrix tests.
  - Pass condition: non-retryable failures never schedule retries.

- `AC-03` Signal dedupe suppresses duplicate execution.
  - Verification: submit duplicate signal payloads.
  - Pass condition: first event creates run, duplicates create `duplicate_event` rows only.

- `AC-04` Fallback hash dedupe works without provider event ID.
  - Verification: identical canonical payload with missing provider ID.
  - Pass condition: duplicates are detected reliably.

- `AC-05` No backfill occurs on signal enable.
  - Verification: enable signal on integration with historical backlog.
  - Pass condition: only new events after enable create runs.

- `AC-06` Validation commands pass.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e:local -- e2e/triggers
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- Timezone handling can drift around DST boundaries.
- Retry classification mistakes can overload integrations or drop recoverable runs.
- Weak canonicalization for fallback hashing can produce false negatives.
- Signal ingestion burst handling may stress dedupe persistence indexes.
````

</details>

````text
# 12 Spec: Debug/Reconciliation Visibility and Reporting Flow

## Goal

Implement the hidden-by-default debug/reconciliation UX, long-running notifications, hard-stop behavior, deep-linking, and optional send-to-developers reporting flow.

## Why This Phase Exists

Reconciliation is intentionally abstracted for non-technical users, but the system still needs reliable observability and intervention controls when recovery runs long.

## In Scope

- Debug/reconciliation visibility controls:
  - hidden by default
  - reveal via `Show Debug Sessions`
  - compact hidden-count indicator
- Long-running notification behavior:
  - threshold trigger
  - repeat cadence
  - reminder actions (`Open debug session`, `Keep running`, `Stop and report`)
- Hard-stop behavior:
  - elapsed-time timeout
  - deterministic failure code
- `Open debug session` deep-link behavior:
  - auto-enable debug visibility in view
  - focus target run/session
- Stop/report flow:
  - field-level preview before consent
  - metadata default
  - allowlisted fields and destinations

## Out of Scope

- New reconciliation algorithm logic.
- Sandboxed execution.
- External crash-reporting infrastructure beyond existing integration paths.

## Baseline Assumptions

- Reconciliation runs are categorized as debug runs by runtime.
- Notification primitives and click-handling exist.
- Reporting upload path can reuse integration dispatch primitives.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Notification surfaces:
  - [context/notification.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/context/notification.tsx)
  - [utils/notification-click.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/utils/notification-click.ts)
- Session/history surfaces:
  - [pages/session.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/pages/session.tsx)
  - [components/titlebar-history.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/components/titlebar-history.ts)
- Settings surfaces:
  - [settings-general.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/components/settings-general.tsx)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Implement Debug Visibility Controls

- Add default hidden behavior for debug runs.
- Add `Show Debug Sessions` persistence and in-view toggle behavior.

### Step 2: Implement Long-Running Reminder Engine

- Trigger first reminder at configured threshold.
- Repeat at configured cadence.
- Include remaining-time text to hard stop.

### Step 3: Implement Hard-Stop Outcome

- Enforce timeout transition with deterministic failure code.
- Keep run/session linkage and audit trail intact.

### Step 4: Implement Deep-Link Behavior

- Ensure `Open debug session` action reveals and focuses target item.

### Step 5: Implement Stop/Report UX

- Add field-level preview.
- Require explicit consent.
- Apply allowlisted fields/destination rules.

### Step 6: Test Coverage

- reminder timing and action behavior
- hard-stop state transition
- deep-link reveal/focus
- report-preview consent constraints

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Debug runs are hidden by default and revealable via filter/setting.
  - Verification: UI tests for default and toggled states.
  - Pass condition: behavior matches setting and in-view toggle contract.

- `AC-02` Long-running reminders follow configured timing and include remaining-time text.
  - Verification: timer-driven integration tests.
  - Pass condition: first and repeated reminders fire at correct cadence with required actions.

- `AC-03` Hard-stop applies deterministically.
  - Verification: simulated long-running debug run.
  - Pass condition: run transitions to timeout failure code at configured hard stop.

- `AC-04` `Open debug session` deep-link auto-reveals and focuses target.
  - Verification: click notification action while debug filter hidden.
  - Pass condition: target run/session is visible and focused.

- `AC-05` Stop/report flow enforces field preview + explicit consent.
  - Verification: report flow e2e with sensitive fields disabled by default.
  - Pass condition: no report dispatch occurs without explicit final consent.

- `AC-06` Validation commands pass.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e:local -- e2e/notifications

- Hidden-by-default debug policy can reduce discoverability if indicators are weak.
- Report flow can accidentally leak fields if allowlist wiring is incomplete.
- Deep-link focus logic can break with list virtualization/pagination.
````

````text
# 13 Spec: Security and Governance Hardening

## Goal

Harden runtime policy enforcement, secret handling, audit completeness, and outbound governance guarantees so behavior is provably safe within this non-sandboxed phase.

## Why This Phase Exists

Core behavior is implemented by earlier phases, but governance-grade guarantees need explicit hardening and adversarial tests to prevent policy bypass and sensitive-data leakage.

## In Scope

- Enforce strict outbound egress policy at runtime.
- Expand secret redaction coverage to:
  - diagnostics
  - crash dumps
  - agent-visible tool output
  - exported reports
- Enforce audit completeness for policy/dispatch decisions.
- Enforce field allowlists for send-to-developers payloads.
- Enforce destination allowlist for report uploads.
- Add explicit tests for bypass attempts and leakage canaries.

## Out of Scope

- Full sandboxing runtime.
- Long-term tamper-evident storage implementation.
- Enterprise/compliance export tooling.

## Baseline Assumptions

- Core draft/dispatch flow is already functional.
- Runtime has centralized dispatcher path for managed integrations.
- Audit event baseline schema is available.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Dispatch/provider integration surfaces:
  - [server/routes/provider.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/provider.ts)
  - [provider/auth.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/provider/auth.ts)
- Diagnostics/logging/runtime surfaces:
  - [server/error.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/server/error.ts)
  - [storage/db.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts)
  - [desktop/src-tauri/src/logging.rs](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/logging.rs)
- App notification/reporting surfaces:
  - [context/notification.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/context/notification.tsx)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Runtime Egress Enforcement

- Centralize managed outbound writes through dispatcher gate.
- Require valid `draft_id` + `integration_attempt_id` for managed endpoint writes.
- Reject direct agent/tool outbound writes to managed endpoints.

### Step 2: Secret Redaction Expansion

- Add redaction utility coverage for logs/diagnostics/tool streams/reports.
- Add canary-based regression tests.

### Step 3: Audit Provenance Enforcement

- Require policy lineage fields for policy/dispatch events.
- Reject incomplete audit writes for applicable event types.

### Step 4: Reporting Privacy Controls

- Enforce allowlisted payload fields.
- Enforce allowlisted report destinations.
- Keep explicit consent and field preview mandatory.

### Step 5: Security Regression Suite

- Add adversarial tests for bypass attempts.
- Add canary secret leakage checks.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Managed outbound writes without valid draft context are rejected.
  - Verification: direct-call bypass tests.
  - Pass condition: writes rejected and audit events emitted.

- `AC-02` Secret canary never appears in diagnostics/crash/tool/report outputs.
  - Verification: canary injection tests across outputs.
  - Pass condition: no output contains unredacted canary value.

- `AC-03` Policy lineage fields are mandatory for policy/dispatch events.
  - Verification: event-write validation tests.
  - Pass condition: incomplete events are rejected deterministically.

- `AC-04` Report payload and destination allowlists are enforced.
  - Verification: attempt non-allowlisted fields/destinations.
  - Pass condition: blocked with explicit reason and no outbound write.

- `AC-05` Non-origin outbound attempts remain hard-blocked.
  - Verification: standard-workspace outbound attempt tests.
  - Pass condition: deterministic `workspace_policy_blocked` with user-visible remediation.

- `AC-06` Validation commands pass.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e:local -- e2e/security
bun run --cwd packages/desktop typecheck
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- Redaction false negatives can silently leak sensitive values.
- Over-redaction can reduce debuggability.
- Strict dispatcher enforcement can break legacy integrations if migration is incomplete.
- Allowlist management can become brittle without clear ownership.
````

</details>

````text
# 14 Spec: End-to-End Hardening and Release Gate

## Goal

Establish a final implementation gate that validates the full feature matrix (manual runs, integration/recovery, drafts/outbound safety, triggers, debug flows, governance controls) with reproducible automated evidence before release.

## Why This Phase Exists

Even with strong per-phase tests, cross-phase interactions can still fail. This phase is the final confidence pass that proves the combined system behaves correctly under realistic and adversarial scenarios.

## In Scope

- Build full e2e matrix for:
  - happy path
  - regression path
  - negative/error path
- Validate restart/crash recovery interactions across integrated phases.
- Validate cross-surface UX coherence:
  - Runs/Operations/Drafts links
  - filter/default behavior
  - notification deep-link behavior
- Validate policy/security enforcement under end-to-end conditions.
- Add release checklist + go/no-go criteria.

## Out of Scope

- New product capabilities.
- New schema/runtime contracts unless critical defect requires fix.
- Large refactors not required for release blocking issues.

## Baseline Assumptions

- Phases `04` through `13` are implemented and logged.
- Local e2e harness is available and stable.
- Test fixtures can simulate trigger/event/recovery scenarios.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- PRD baseline:
  - [baseline.prd.draft.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/baseline.prd.draft.md)
- Spec baseline:
  - [04.runtime-contracts-and-state-machines.spec.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/04.runtime-contracts-and-state-machines.spec.md)
  - [05.jj-runtime-foundation.spec.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/05.jj-runtime-foundation.spec.md)
  - [06.workflow-library-authoring-and-validation.spec.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/06.workflow-library-authoring-and-validation.spec.md)
  - [07.manual-run-execution.spec.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/07.manual-run-execution.spec.md)
  - [08.integration-queue-and-recovery.spec.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/08.integration-queue-and-recovery.spec.md)
  - [09.history-runs-operations-ui.spec.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/09.history-runs-operations-ui.spec.md)
  - [10.drafts-dispatch-and-outbound-gates.spec.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/10.drafts-dispatch-and-outbound-gates.spec.md)
  - [11.cron-signal-trigger-engine.spec.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/11.cron-signal-trigger-engine.spec.md)
  - [12.debug-reconciliation-and-reporting.spec.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/12.debug-reconciliation-and-reporting.spec.md)
  - [13.security-governance-hardening.spec.md](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/13.security-governance-hardening.spec.md)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Build Unified E2E Scenario Matrix

- Create traceable matrix mapping each PRD invariant to one or more e2e tests.
- Mark test IDs and expected assertions.

### Step 2: Implement Missing E2E Cases

- Add missing cases discovered in matrix coverage gap analysis.
- Prioritize negative/error and restart/recovery paths.

### Step 3: Run Full Validation Sweep

- Run package-level unit/typecheck suites.
- Run local e2e harness suites by scenario group.
- Capture deterministic artifacts for failures.

### Step 4: Release Gate Decision Pack

- Produce pass/fail summary.
- List blocking defects by severity and owning phase.
- Confirm go/no-go with explicit criteria.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Every major PRD invariant has at least one automated e2e assertion.
  - Verification: matrix audit.
  - Pass condition: no uncovered critical invariants.

- `AC-02` Full lifecycle scenarios pass:
  - manual run -> integration -> history links
  - draft approve/send/reject/blocked flows
  - cron missed slot + retries
  - signal dedupe/no-backfill
  - debug reminder -> deep-link -> hard-stop path
  - non-origin outbound block
  - crash/restart recovery in integration
  - Verification: end-to-end suite.
  - Pass condition: all scenarios pass in local harness.

- `AC-03` Security/governance adversarial tests pass.
  - Verification: bypass/leakage canary suite.
  - Pass condition: no bypass and no secret leakage.

- `AC-04` No critical/high open defects remain after validation run.
  - Verification: consolidated defect report.
  - Pass condition: zero unresolved critical/high items.

- `AC-05` Release gate command suite is green.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e:local -- e2e
bun run --cwd packages/desktop typecheck
bun ./packages/sdk/js/script/build.ts
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass and SDK generation leaves no unexpected drift.

## Risk Register

- E2E flakiness can mask real regressions or create false alarms.
- Recovery scenario simulation may miss platform-specific timing behavior.
- Final gate can balloon if prior phases under-test negative paths.
- Late-found defects may require cross-phase hotfix coordination.
````

````text
- Reject or ignore events at/before enable timestamp (`event_time > signal_enabled_at`).

### Step 3: Trigger Metadata and Session Creation

- Ensure each automated run creates a new session.
- Persist trigger metadata and linkage IDs.

### Step 4: Notification/Visibility Integration

- Wire missed/duplicate outcomes into Runs surface and notifications.

### Step 5: Test Coverage

- cron scheduling and missed-slot
- retry classification
- signal dedupe and fallback hash
- no-backfill behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Missed cron slots create deterministic skipped outcomes.
  - Verification: simulate offline/missed interval.
  - Pass condition: one `skipped` run row per missed slot with reason metadata and notification.

- `AC-02` Retry policy applies only to retryable classes.
  - Verification: failure-class matrix tests.
  - Pass condition: non-retryable failures never schedule retries.

- `AC-03` Signal dedupe suppresses duplicate execution.
  - Verification: submit duplicate signal payloads.
  - Pass condition: first event creates run, duplicates create `duplicate_event` rows only.

- `AC-04` Fallback hash dedupe works without provider event ID.
  - Verification: identical canonical payload with missing provider ID.
  - Pass condition: duplicates are detected reliably.

- `AC-05` No backfill occurs on signal enable.
  - Verification: enable signal on integration with historical backlog.
  - Pass condition: only new events after enable create runs.

- `AC-06` Validation commands pass.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e:local -- e2e/triggers
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- Timezone handling can drift around DST boundaries.
- Retry classification mistakes can overload integrations or drop recoverable runs.
- Weak canonicalization for fallback hashing can produce false negatives.
- Signal ingestion burst handling may stress dedupe persistence indexes.
````

</details>

<details>
<summary>Command output</summary>

````text
# 07 Spec: Manual Run Execution

## Goal

Ship end-to-end manual workflow execution with session linkage and deterministic run lifecycle transitions, without yet introducing queued integration or automated triggers.

## Why This Phase Exists

This is the first user-visible execution slice. It validates the run model and session behavior before concurrency/recovery complexity is added.

## In Scope

- Manual trigger entrypoint for workflows.
- Create one new chat session per top-level run.
- Execute run in isolated run workspace.
- End-of-run validation flow:
  - success path
  - invalid-output repair loop in same session
- No-change vs change outcome classification.
- Persist run records with canonical lifecycle states.
- Persist integration-ready candidate metadata when file changes exist.
- Final operation linkage commit remains out of scope for this phase and is owned by Phase 08.
- Always perform run workspace cleanup on terminal outcomes.

## Out of Scope

- Integration queue/serialization and replay logic.
- Draft creation/dispatch.
- Cron and signal triggers.
- Hidden debug/reconciliation notification UX.

## Baseline Assumptions

- Runtime contracts (Phase 04) and JJ foundation (Phase 05) are available.
- Workflow/library validation (Phase 06) can mark runnable/non-runnable before execution.
- Existing session creation and prompt plumbing can be reused.
- Duplicate manual-trigger requests are deduplicated or rejected deterministically.
- If cleanup fails, run terminal state remains stable and `cleanup_failed` is recorded for janitor retry.

## Required Keep Set

Not applicable.

## Deletion Candidates

Not applicable.

## Evidence Anchors

- Session processing surfaces:
  - [session/processor.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts)
  - [session/index.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/opencode/src/session/index.ts)
- Prompt submit flow:
  - [prompt-input/submit.ts](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/components/prompt-input/submit.ts)
- Session UI/route surfaces:
  - [pages/session.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/pages/session.tsx)
  - [pages/layout.tsx](/Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/packages/app/src/pages/layout.tsx)

## Agent Execution Plan

### Step 1: Add Manual Run Start Endpoint/Command

- Validate workflow is runnable.
- Create run record + linked session.
- Create isolated run workspace.

### Step 2: Execute Run in Session Context

- Run top-level instructions via base orchestration flow.
- Keep context in same run session thread.

### Step 3: Validation and Repair Loop

- Run final validation checks.
- If invalid, prompt same session for repair and retry validation.
- Keep retry attempts auditable in run events.
- Apply deterministic repair-loop exhaustion policy (attempt/time cap + terminal failure code).

### Step 4: Persist Outcome and Cleanup

- If no file changes: `completed_no_change`, no operation row.
- If file changes: transition to `ready_for_integration` and persist integration-ready candidate metadata only.
- Cleanup run workspace for all terminal outcomes.

### Step 5: Test Coverage

- Manual run happy path.
- Validation-fail then repair path.
- No-change path.
- Cleanup on success/failure/cancel.
- Workspace isolation boundaries.
- Cancel during `running`/`validating`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AC-01` Manual run creation always creates a new linked session.
  - Verification: run start test asserts distinct `session_id` per run.
  - Pass condition: each manual run has a new session thread.

- `AC-02` Run lifecycle transitions are legal and deterministic.
  - Verification: unit/integration tests around explicit phase contract.
  - Pass condition: transition path is deterministic:
    - `queued -> running -> validating -> completed_no_change`
    - `queued -> running -> validating -> ready_for_integration`
    - `queued -> running|validating -> failed|canceled`

- `AC-03` Validation-fail path repairs in same session.
  - Verification: test fixture forcing invalid final state.
  - Pass condition: repair prompt/events occur in same `session_id`; exhaustion policy yields deterministic failure code when cap is hit.

- `AC-04` No-change runs do not create Operations entries.
  - Verification: run with no file mutations.
  - Pass condition: run ends `completed_no_change` and operations query returns zero new rows.

- `AC-05` Change-path runs persist integration-ready candidate metadata and do not finalize operations in this phase.
  - Verification: run with file mutations.
  - Pass condition: run ends `ready_for_integration` with required metadata and no finalized operation linkage row.

- `AC-06` All terminal outcomes trigger cleanup.
  - Verification: success/failure/cancel test matrix.
  - Pass condition: no orphaned run workspace remains.

- `AC-07` Manual run execution is isolated to run workspace boundaries.
  - Verification: run integration test with filesystem assertions.
  - Pass condition: mutations occur only inside run workspace until later integration phases.

- `AC-08` Validation commands pass.
  - Verification commands:
```bash
bun run --cwd packages/opencode typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/opencode test
bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck
bun run --cwd packages/app test:unit
bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e:local -- e2e/session
```
  - Pass condition: all commands pass.

## Risk Register

- Repair loops can become noisy if validation conditions are too broad.
- Session linkage bugs can create orphan run/session records.
- Cleanup race conditions can leave stale run workspaces.
- Early execution path may expose latent prompt/tool regressions.
````

</details>

```text
# Convergence Review v2 (Critical/High Only)

## Phase 04 — `04.runtime-contracts-and-state-machines.spec.md`

1. **High**: The spec requires an "explicit in-spec transition matrix" but does not include one.
- Refs: `107`, `131-133`.
- Why it is critical/high: legal edges remain open to interpretation, so implementations can diverge while still passing generic tests.

2. **High**: The spec says canonical schema contract "must explicitly list" columns/constraints/indexes, but those concrete lists are not present.
- Refs: `37-42`.
- Why it is critical/high: migration and constraint implementation can vary by engineer, causing incompatible persistence semantics.

## Phase 07 — `07.manual-run-execution.spec.md`

1. **High**: Repair-loop exhaustion policy is required but not concretely defined (no cap values, precedence, or canonical failure code).
- Refs: `77`, `109`.
- Why it is critical/high: loop termination and failure outcomes are non-deterministic.

2. **High**: Duplicate manual-trigger behavior is ambiguous (`deduplicated or rejected deterministically`) without a single required policy.
- Refs: `37`.
- Why it is critical/high: same user action can produce different run/session creation outcomes.

3. **High**: Partial-start failure contract is missing for run/session/workspace creation sequence.
- Refs: `63-65`.
- Why it is critical/high: orphan rows or partially created resources may occur with inconsistent rollback behavior.

## Phase 11 — `11.cron-signal-trigger-engine.spec.md`

1. **High**: Missed-slot cap/coalescing is required but policy is not specified (threshold, grouping, emitted records).
- Refs: `16`, `72`, `100-103`.
- Why it is critical/high: long-offline recovery behavior is undefined and test outcomes can differ across implementations.

2. **High**: DST behavior is declared deterministic, but concrete spring-forward/fall-back rules are not defined.
- Refs: `17`, `73`, `104-107`.
- Why it is critical/high: schedule execution can differ by runtime/library interpretation.

3. **High**: Retry classification is referenced, but the canonical failure-class -> retryability -> failure-code mapping is not enumerated in-spec.
- Refs: `18`, `108-110`.
- Why it is critical/high: retry/no-retry decisions may vary for the same error class.

4. **High**: Fallback dedupe hash uses "canonical adapter fields" without a required canonicalization/version contract.
- Refs: `24`, `78`, `116-118`.
- Why it is critical/high: hash stability is implementation-dependent; duplicates may be inconsistently detected.
```

</details>

```text
# Convergence Review v2: Phase 10 and 13

## Phase 10: `10.drafts-dispatch-and-outbound-gates.spec.md`

### Findings

- High: managed endpoint inventory fail-closed behavior is required but not explicitly acceptance-tested.
  - Refs: `L31-L32`, `L94-L98`, `L113-L155`
  - Ambiguity: the spec requires an adapter-owned endpoint inventory, but no AC explicitly verifies that outbound writes to endpoints not present in the inventory are rejected.

- High: default-deny behavior for policy-evaluation failure is required in plan text but not directly acceptance-tested.
  - Refs: `L101-L104`, `L127-L130`, `L143-L150`
  - Ambiguity: policy-eval failure (`blocked` default-deny) is a core safety rule, but AC coverage focuses on revalidation after approval and does not explicitly require a failing policy-eval path at decision time.

## Phase 13: `13.security-governance-hardening.spec.md`

### Findings

- High: fail-closed linkage between audit-provenance failure and outbound dispatch stop is not explicitly acceptance-tested.
  - Refs: `L74-L77`, `L103-L106`
  - Ambiguity: step plan requires no outbound dispatch when lineage is missing, but AC-04 only asserts incomplete events are rejected; it does not explicitly assert dispatch is blocked with zero side effects.

- High: managed endpoint inventory enforcement still lacks an explicit negative AC.
  - Refs: `L14`, `L62-L66`, `L91-L98`
  - Ambiguity: runtime enforcement requires endpoint inventory matching, but ACs do not explicitly verify rejection for outbound attempts targeting endpoints outside inventory.
```

<details>
<summary>Patch input</summary>

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/04.runtime-contracts-and-state-machines.spec.md
@@
 Canonical schema contract in this phase must explicitly list:
 
 - columns per entity (`run`, `operation`, `draft`, `integration_attempt`, `audit_event`)
 - constraints (unique, nullable, default, foreign-key expectations)
 - indexes and query intent
+
+Baseline column/constraint floor for this phase:
+
+- `run`:
+  - `id` (uuidv7, pk)
+  - `status` (enum, indexed)
+  - `trigger_type`
+  - `workspace_id`
+  - `session_id`
+  - `ready_for_integration_at` (immutable after first set)
+  - `failure_code` / `reason_code`
+  - timestamps (`created_at`, `updated_at`, `started_at`, `finished_at`)
+- `operation`:
+  - `id` (uuidv7, pk)
+  - `run_id` (fk)
+  - `workspace_id`
+  - `status`
+  - `source_operation_id` (nullable fk for revert linkage)
+  - JJ linkage fields from PRD
+- `draft`:
+  - `id` (uuidv7, pk)
+  - `run_id` (nullable fk)
+  - `workspace_id`
+  - `status`
+  - `integration_id`
+  - policy lineage fields
+  - timestamps
+- `integration_attempt`:
+  - `id` (`integration_attempt_id`, pk)
+  - `run_id`
+  - `workspace_id`
+  - `state` (`attempt_created`, `jj_applied`, `db_linked`, `finalized`)
+  - `replay_index`
+  - unique `(run_id, id)`
+- `audit_event`:
+  - `id` (uuidv7, pk)
+  - `event_type`, `actor_type`, `occurred_at`
+  - linkage IDs (`workspace_id`, `run_id`, `operation_id`, `draft_id`, `integration_attempt_id`)
+  - policy lineage fields (required for policy/dispatch events)
+  - typed `event_payload`
@@
 ### Step 3: Add Transition Guard Service
 
 - Add centralized transition function per entity.
 - Reject illegal transitions with machine-readable errors.
 - Record mandatory terminal metadata (`reason`, `failure_code`, timestamps).
 - Check legal edges against an explicit in-spec transition matrix and keep matrix synced with tests.
+
+Canonical legal transition matrix in this phase:
+
+- `run.status`:
+  - `queued -> running|canceled`
+  - `running -> validating|failed|canceled`
+  - `validating -> ready_for_integration|completed_no_change|failed|canceled`
+  - `ready_for_integration -> integrating|canceled`
+  - `integrating -> reconciling|completed|failed|cancel_requested`
+  - `reconciling -> integrating|failed|cancel_requested`
+  - `cancel_requested -> completed|canceled|failed`
+  - `completed|completed_no_change|failed|canceled|skipped` are terminal
+- `operation.status`:
+  - `completed -> reverted`
+  - `reverted` terminal
+- `draft.status`:
+  - `pending -> approved|auto_approved|blocked|rejected`
+  - `approved|auto_approved -> sent|failed|blocked|pending` (pending on material edit invalidation)
+  - `blocked -> pending|rejected|failed`
+  - `sent|rejected|failed` terminal
*** End Patch
```

</details>

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/polarzero/code/projects/origin/docs/specs/11.cron-signal-trigger-engine.spec.md
@@
 - Trigger failure classes map to canonical machine-readable failure codes.
 - Dedupe retention horizon (minimum 30 days) is greater than expected provider replay windows for this phase.
+- Missed-slot coalescing policy in this phase:
+  - generate at most 100 skipped rows per scheduler catch-up cycle
+  - if more than 100 slots are missed, write one coalesced skipped summary row for the overflow
+- DST policy in this phase:
+  - spring-forward: nonexistent local-time slots are recorded as skipped (`dst_gap_skipped`)
+  - fall-back: duplicated local-time slots are evaluated by unique UTC schedule boundaries (can yield two executions where applicable)
+- Retry-class mapping in this phase:
+  - retryable: `integration_transport_error`, `transient_runtime_error`, `integration_timeout`
+  - non-retryable: `validation_error`, `schema_error`, `policy_blocked`, `workspace_policy_blocked`
+- Fallback hash canonicalization contract:
+  - canonical JSON with lexicographically sorted keys
+  - UTF-8 encoding
+  - adapter-owned field allowlist version tag included in hash input
@@
 - Apply missed-slot cap/coalescing when offline gap would create excessive skipped rows.
 - Implement explicit DST boundary behavior (spring-forward/fall-back).
+- Emit deterministic overflow summary when coalescing is applied.
@@
 - If provider event ID absent, compute fallback hash from canonical adapter fields.
+- Fallback hash canonicalization uses sorted-key JSON + UTF-8 + adapter field-set version tag.
 - Duplicate event records no run execution and creates visible duplicate-event row.
@@

   - Verification: simulate offline/missed interval.
-  - Pass condition: deterministic skipped outcomes follow cap/coalescing policy with reason metadata and notification.
+  - Pass condition: deterministic skipped outcomes follow cap/coalescing policy; overflow emits one coalesced skipped summary row.
@@

-  - Pass condition: run/skip behavior matches documented cron-time contract.
+  - Pass condition: spring-forward and fall-back outcomes match documented DST policy and codes.
@@
 - `AC-03` Retry policy applies only to retryable classes and canonical failure-code mapping.
   - Verification: failure-class matrix tests.
-  - Pass condition: non-retryable failures never schedule retries and retryable classes map to expected failure codes.
+  - Pass condition: retryability strictly matches declared class map and failure-code mapping.
@@

-  - Pass condition: duplicates are detected reliably.
+  - Pass condition: duplicates are detected reliably and hash stability is invariant to JSON key ordering.
```
